canonicalization.json: RFC8259 JSON with sort_keys=true, separators=(',', ':'), ensure_ascii=true
canonicalization.ordering: tools, prompts, resources, and public-discovery entries are sorted by stable public identifiers; object keys are sorted recursively by JSON encoder
canonicalization.unicode: metadata strings are hashed exactly as emitted by FastMCP/Pydantic after explicit prompt-argument redaction

generated_from.mcp_get_prompt: live FastMCP mcp.get_prompt() template text with synthetic argument values redacted to placeholders
generated_from.mcp_list_prompts: live FastMCP mcp.list_prompts() public prompt metadata
generated_from.mcp_list_resource_templates: live FastMCP mcp.list_resource_templates() public resource template metadata only
generated_from.mcp_list_resources: live FastMCP mcp.list_resources() public resource metadata only; resource payloads are not read or hashed
generated_from.public_discovery: allowlisted /.well-known/mcp-server.json capability names plus README/docs mention checks

arguments.description: MCP HTTP endpoint expected by VS Code or Copilot.
arguments.name: endpoint

list_prompts.description: Check VS Code/devcontainer MCP setup, forwarded ports, health endpoints, auth mode, and local model service state.
list_prompts.name: devcontainer_health_check
list_prompts.title: Devcontainer health check

template.description: Check VS Code/devcontainer MCP setup, forwarded ports, health endpoints, auth mode, and local model service state.

content.text:
# Devcontainer health check

Goal: Verify that VS Code/Copilot can discover and use the MCP server at `<argument:endpoint>` from the devcontainer workflow.

Use this MCP workflow through the public `task_router` entrypoint. Prefer `mode='task'` for natural-language orchestration; when a client exposes internal workflow names in summaries or reports, keep the chain aligned with:
- `task_router(mode='status')`
- `healthz`
- `docs/vscode-mcp-onboarding.md`

Safety guardrails:
- Never echo bearer token values; only report whether a token/header is configured.
- Keep auth enabled unless the user intentionally selected documented insecure local-only mode.
- Treat port and Ollama checks as diagnostics, not permission to change host services.

Return:
- Discovery status for MCP prompts/tools/resources in VS Code or Copilot.
- Health summary for `/healthz`, `/mcp`, port forwarding, auth mode, and Ollama.
- Next actions with exact docs links or commands, redacting secrets.

arguments.description: Readiness summary detail, usually quick or full.
arguments.name: summary_mode

list_prompts.description: Prepare a release gate report using existing readiness, impact, docs, license, risk, security, and test checks.
list_prompts.name: release_readiness_check
list_prompts.title: Release readiness check

template.description: Prepare a release gate report using existing readiness, impact, docs, license, risk, security, and test checks.

content.text:
# Release readiness check

Goal: Assess whether `<argument:head_ref>` is ready to release against `<argument:base_ref>` with `<argument:summary_mode>` reporting.

Use this MCP workflow through the public `task_router` entrypoint. Prefer `mode='task'` for natural-language orchestration; when a client exposes internal workflow names in summaries or reports, keep the chain aligned with:
- `clarification_gate(operation='release_readiness')`
- `quality_router(mode='release_readiness')`
- `release_readiness`
- `required_tool_chain`

Safety guardrails:
- Run or inspect clarification_gate first; if it returns ok_to_continue=false, render its fallback_checklist or MCP elicitation request instead of recommending release action.
- Do not bypass failing gates; report blockers clearly.
- Do not mutate files unless the user explicitly requests a follow-up fix workflow and ALLOW_MUTATIONS permits it.
- Keep artifacts compatible with existing structured readiness reports.

Return:
- Release decision: ready, not ready, or needs human review.
- Gate-by-gate summary for tests, docs, license, impact, risk, and security.
- Required follow-up tool chain or validation commands.

arguments.description: Base Git ref for the comparison, for example origin/main or HEAD~1.
arguments.name: base_ref

arguments.description: Optional review focus such as security, docs, tests, or API compatibility.
arguments.name: focus

list_prompts.description: Review the current branch diff with impact, risk, and test guidance without mutating files.
list_prompts.name: review_changed_files
list_prompts.title: Review changed files

template.description: Review the current branch diff with impact, risk, and test guidance without mutating files.

content.text:
# Review changed files

Goal: Compare the working branch against `<argument:base_ref>` and produce concise review findings for changed files. Focus area: <argument:focus>.

Use this MCP workflow through the public `task_router` entrypoint. Prefer `mode='task'` for natural-language orchestration; when a client exposes internal workflow names in summaries or reports, keep the chain aligned with:
- `task_router(mode='task', task='review')`
- `change_impact_gate`
- `quality_router(mode='change_impact')`

Safety guardrails:
- Stay read-only; do not edit files or run mutation modes.
- Respect existing mutation and security gates if you later recommend fixes.
- Consult `test_impact_map`/`impact_tests` before mutation or release-readiness checks; report selected tests and unmapped coverage gaps.
- Call out high-risk files, missing tests, and rollback considerations before suggestions.

Return:
- Findings first, each with file/path evidence when possible.
- Changed-file risk summary and recommended validation commands.
- Explicit note if no blocking issue is found.

arguments.description: File, directory, diff range, dependency, or feature area to triage.
arguments.name: target

list_prompts.description: Triage suspicious files, diffs, or dependencies through existing security/risk workflows without weakening gates.
list_prompts.name: security_triage
list_prompts.title: Security triage

template.description: Triage suspicious files, diffs, or dependencies through existing security/risk workflows without weakening gates.

content.text:
# Security triage

Goal: Investigate security-sensitive behavior in `<argument:target>` and separate confirmed findings from hypotheses.

Use this MCP workflow through the public `task_router` entrypoint. Prefer `mode='task'` for natural-language orchestration; when a client exposes internal workflow names in summaries or reports, keep the chain aligned with:
- `task_router(mode='task', task='security')`
- `change_impact_gate`
- `policy_simulator`

Safety guardrails:
- Prefer read-only inspection and minimal reproduction steps.
- Do not print secrets, tokens, private keys, or credential material.
- Do not disable authentication, sandboxing, policy, or mutation gates.

Return:
- Prioritized findings with exploitability and affected paths.
- Safe verification steps that avoid secret exposure.
- Mitigation options and residual risk.

arguments.description: Short description of the intended refactor.
arguments.name: refactor_goal

list_prompts.description: Plan a safe pre-refactor snapshot and rollback path before any mutation workflow starts.
list_prompts.name: snapshot_before_refactor
list_prompts.title: Snapshot before refactor

template.description: Plan a safe pre-refactor snapshot and rollback path before any mutation workflow starts.

content.text:
# Snapshot before refactor

Goal: Before starting `<argument:refactor_goal>`, create a verifiable rollback point and summarize the safe mutation plan.

Use this MCP workflow through the public `task_router` entrypoint. Prefer `mode='task'` for natural-language orchestration; when a client exposes internal workflow names in summaries or reports, keep the chain aligned with:
- `workspace_transaction(mode='snapshot')`
- `workspace_transaction(mode='restore')`
- `quality_router(mode='self_check')`

Safety guardrails:
- Only create snapshots or mutate files when ALLOW_MUTATIONS and user intent permit it.
- Record snapshot id and current Git status before edits.
- Prefer small, reviewable edits and run validation before handing off.

Return:
- Snapshot/rollback plan and whether mutation is currently allowed.
- Refactor steps ordered from safest to riskiest.
- Validation commands and restore instructions if the refactor fails.

dry_run.description: Prepare policy/context/digests without sending a sampling/createMessage request.
dry_run.title: Dry Run

execution_mode.description: Agent execution profile (`auto`, `online`, or `offline`). Offline mode is explicit metadata; clients must keep sampling local/onboard if privacy requires it.
execution_mode.title: Execution Mode

max_bytes.description: Optional per-call byte cap, clamped by MCP_SAMPLING_MAX_BYTES.
max_bytes.title: Max Bytes

max_context_tokens.description: Optional estimated context-token cap, clamped by MCP_SAMPLING_MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS.
max_context_tokens.title: Max Context Tokens

max_paths.description: Optional per-call path cap, clamped by MCP_SAMPLING_MAX_PATHS.
max_paths.title: Max Paths

max_tokens.description: Optional generated-token cap, clamped by MCP_SAMPLING_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS.
max_tokens.title: Max Tokens

paths.description: Repository-relative files to summarize/classify. Defaults to small repository overview docs. Secret-bearing paths and paths outside REPO_PATH are denied/omitted.
paths.title: Paths

purpose.description: Allowed MCP sampling purpose: summary, classification, or workflow_selection. Mutations, release approval, and security conclusions are denied.
purpose.title: Purpose

question.description: Optional bounded question. Secret-looking values and host absolute paths are redacted before any client-mediated sampling request.
question.title: Question

timeout_seconds.description: Client-mediated sampling timeout in seconds; capped at 30 seconds.
timeout_seconds.title: Timeout Seconds

list_tools.description: Return compact structured diff summary with risk hints.
list_tools.name: summarize_diff

list_tools.description: Single public task router for LLM agents. Default `mode='task'` is the normal entrypoint. Use `mode='workflow_select'` plus `execution_mode` for read-only, mode-aware workflow-card retrieval before choosing a workflow. Explicit modes expose status|embed|infer|parallel_infer|autocomplete|rerank|coding_infer|coding_check|coding_pip|coding_sandbox|workflow_select.

auto_parallel_when_possible.description: Whether `mode='infer'` should automatically upgrade independent prompt batches to `parallel_infer`.
auto_parallel_when_possible.title: Auto Parallel When Possible

backend.description: Execution backend. Common values include `auto`, `endpoint`, `fallback`, `hash`, and `rule`, depending on the selected mode.
backend.title: Backend

check_profile.description: Coding check profile for `coding_check` or `coding_infer` with `run_checks=true`.
check_profile.title: Check Profile

check_target.description: File or directory target for coding checks.
check_target.title: Check Target

check_timeout_seconds.description: Timeout in seconds for coding checks and package installs.
check_timeout_seconds.title: Check Timeout Seconds

compress.description: Whether to compress large tabular or list results in supported modes.
compress.title: Compress

execution_mode.description: Agent execution profile for workflow selection: `auto`, `online`/`cloud-assisted`, or `offline`/`onboard-only`. Used by `mode='workflow_select'` to expose mode-aware workflow-card routing without creating a second selector.
execution_mode.title: Execution Mode

max_parallel.description: Maximum concurrent workers for `parallel_infer` or inferred auto-parallel batches.
max_parallel.title: Max Parallel

max_tokens.description: Maximum generated tokens for inference-style modes.
max_tokens.title: Max Tokens

memory_session.description: Optional session key for `mode='task'`. Reuse the same value across related requests to carry compact task/session memory; empty becomes `default`.
memory_session.title: Memory Session

mode.description: Execution mode. Start with `task` for almost every natural-language request; it classifies the request, injects compact task/session memory, and dispatches to the right specialist flow. Use `workflow_select` first when you are unsure which existing MCP workflow/prompt/tool to use. Use the other modes only when you intentionally need raw status, infer, embed, rerank, autocomplete, or coding sandbox/check/package behavior.
mode.title: Mode

model.description: Explicit model override. Leave empty to use router-selected or default models.
model.title: Model

normalize.description: Whether embeddings should be L2-normalized in `mode='embed'`.
normalize.title: Normalize

output_profile.description: Output verbosity/profile. Common values are `compact`, `normal`, and `verbose`.
output_profile.title: Output Profile

pip_upgrade.description: Whether `coding_pip` should install with upgrade semantics.
pip_upgrade.title: Pip Upgrade

prompt.description: Primary request text for the default `task` flow, `infer`, and `coding_infer`.
prompt.title: Prompt

prompts.description: Independent request batch for `parallel_infer`, or an explicit batch or single-prompt override for `infer`.
prompts.title: Prompts

query.description: Query text for reranking and other mode-specific router operations.
query.title: Query

sandbox_id.description: Existing sandbox identifier for coding sandbox reuse or management.
sandbox_id.title: Sandbox Id

sandbox_mode.description: Coding sandbox mode. Use `shared` to reuse the shared sandbox or another supported mode for isolation.
sandbox_mode.title: Sandbox Mode

store_result.description: Whether to store the result in the server-side result handle cache.
store_result.title: Store Result

suffix.description: Autocomplete suffix text after the cursor when `mode='autocomplete'`.
suffix.title: Suffix

system.description: Optional system prompt override for inference-style modes.
system.title: System

task.description: Task hint such as `general`, `coding`, `micro_coding`, `review`, or `security`; used for routing and fallback prompt shaping.
task.title: Task

top_k.description: Maximum results to return in ranked modes. Defaults to 3 for `mode='workflow_select'` and 20 for `mode='rerank'`.
top_k.title: Top K
